volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Trojan Women (Section 870 - Section 1.1070)

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: ὄνομα δάμαρτος ἥ ποτʼ ἦν ἐμὴ λέγω —
sections.translation: to call her , though she once was mine — for now she is one among the other Trojan women who share these tents as captives . For they , the very men who who toiled to take her with the spear , have granted to me to slay her ,

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: θέλοιμʼ ἄγεσθαι πάλιν ἐς Ἀργείαν χθόνα .
sections.translation: or , if I will , to spare and carry back with me to Argos . Now my purpose is not to put her to death in Troy , but to carry her to Hellas in my sea-borne ship , and then surrender her to death , a recompense to all whose friends were slain in Ilium .

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶα χωρεῖτʼ ἐς δόμους , ὀπάονες ,
sections.translation: Ho ! my servants , enter the tent , and drag her out to me by her hair foul with murder ; and when a favoring breeze shall blow , to Hellas will we convey her .

sections.label: Section 884
sections.original: ὦ γῆς ὄχημα κἀπὶ γῆς ἔχων ἕδραν ,
sections.translation: O you that do support the earth and rest thereupon ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ὅστις ποτʼ εἶ σύ , δυστόπαστος εἰδέναι ,
sections.translation: whoever you are , a riddle past our knowledge ! Zeus , owhether you are natural necessity , or man ’ s intellect , to you I pray ; for , though you tread over a noiseless path , all your dealings with mankind are guided by justice .

sections.label: Section 889
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; εὐχὰς ὡς ἐκαίνισας θεῶν .
sections.translation: What is this ? Strange the prayer you offer to the gods !

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: αἰνῶ σε , Μενέλαʼ , εἰ κτενεῖς δάμαρτα σήν .
sections.translation: I thank you , Menelaus , if you will slay that wife of yours . Yet shun the sight of her , lest she strike you with longing . For she ensnares the eyes of men , overthrows their towns , and burns their houses , so potent are her witcheries ! Well I know her ; so do you and those her victims too .

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: Μενέλαε , φροίμιον μὲν ἄξιον φόβου
sections.translation: Menelaus ! this prelude well may fill me with alarm ; for I am taken with violence by your servants ’ hands and brought before these tents . Still , though I am sure you hate me , yet I want to inquire

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: Ἕλλησι καὶ σοὶ τῆς ἐμῆς ψυχῆς πέρι ;
sections.translation: what you and Hellas have decided about my life .

sections.label: Section 901
sections.original: οὐκ εἰς ἀκριβὲς ἦλθες , ἀλλʼ ἅπας στρατὸς
sections.translation: To judge your case required no great exactness ; the army with one consent , that army whom you wronged , handed you over to me to die .

sections.label: Section 903
sections.original: ἔξεστιν οὖν πρὸς ταῦτʼ ἀμείψασθαι λόγῳ ,
sections.translation: May I answer this decision , proving that my death , if I am to die , will be unjust ?

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: οὐκ ἐς λόγους ἐλήλυθʼ , ἀλλά σε κτενῶν .
sections.translation: I came not to argue , but to slay you .

sections.label: Section 906
sections.original: ἄκουσον αὐτῆς , μὴ θάνῃ τοῦδʼ ἐνδεής ,
sections.translation: Hear her , Menelaus ; let her not die for want of that , and let me answer her again , for you know nothing of her villainies in Troy ; and the whole case , if summed up ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κτενεῖ νιν οὕτως ὥστε μηδαμοῦ φυγεῖν .
sections.translation: will insure her death against all chance of an escape .

sections.label: Section 911
sections.original: σχολῆς τὸ δῶρον · εἰ δὲ βούλεται λέγειν ,
sections.translation: This gift needs leisure ; still , if she wishes to speak , she may . Yet I will grant her this because of your words , that she may hear them , and not for her own sake .

sections.label: Section 913
sections.original: δώσω τόδʼ αὐτῇ · τῆσδε δʼ οὐ δώσω χάριν .
sections.translation: Perhaps you will not answer me , from counting me a foe ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: οὐκ ἀνταμείψῃ πολεμίαν ἡγούμενος .
sections.translation: whether my words seem good or ill . Yet I will put my charges and yours over against each other , and then reply to the accusations I suppose you will advance against me . First , then , that woman was the author of these troubles

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: Πάριν τεκοῦσα · δεύτερον δʼ ἀπώλεσε
sections.translation: by giving birth to Paris ; next , old Priam ruined Troy and me , because he did not slay his child Alexander , baleful semblance of a fire-brand , [*] long ago . Hear what followed . This man was to judge the claims of three rival goddesses ;

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: καὶ Παλλάδος μὲν ἦν Ἀλεξάνδρῳ δόσις
sections.translation: so Pallas offered him command of all the Phrygians , and the destruction of Hellas ; Hera promised he should spread his dominion over Asia , and the utmost bounds of Europe , if he would decide for her ; but Cypris spoke in rapture of my loveliness ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: δώσειν ὑπέσχετʼ , εἰ θεὰς ὑπερδράμοι
sections.translation: and promised him this gift , if she should have the preference over those two for beauty . Now mark the inference I deduce from this ; Cypris won the day over the goddesses , and thus far has my marriage proved of benefit to Hellas , that you are not subject to barbarian rule , neither vanquished in the strife , nor yet by tyrants crushed .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ἃ δʼ εὐτύχησεν Ἑλλάς , ὠλόμην ἐγὼ
sections.translation: What Hellas gained , was ruin to me , sold for my beauty , and now I am reproached for that which should have set a crown upon my head . But you will say I am silent on the real matter at hand , how it was I started forth and left your house by stealth .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ἦλθʼ οὐχὶ μικρὰν θεὸν ἔχων αὑτοῦ μέτα
sections.translation: With no small goddess at his side he came , my evil genius , call him Alexander or Paris , as you will ; and you , villain , left him behind in your house , and sailed away from Sparta to the land of Crete .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: εἶἑν .
sections.translation: Enough of this ! For all that followed I must question myself , not you ; what thought led me to follow the stranger from your house , traitress to my country and my home ? Punish the goddess , show yourself more mighty even than Zeus , who , though he lords it over the other gods ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: κείνης δὲ δοῦλός ἐστι · συγγνώμη δʼ ἐμοί .
sections.translation: is her slave ; therefore I may well be pardoned . Still , from this you might draw a specious argument against me ; when Paris died , and earth concealed his corpse , I should have left his house and sought the Argive fleet , since my marriage was no longer in the hands of gods .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ἔσπευδον αὐτὸ τοῦτο · μάρτυρες δέ μοι
sections.translation: That was what I was eager to do ; and the warders on the towers and watchmen on the walls can bear me witness , for often they found me seeking to let myself down stealthily by cords from the battlements but there was that new husband , Deiphobus , that carried me off

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: Δηίφοβος ἄλοχον εἶχεν ἀκόντων Φρυγῶν .
sections.translation: by force to be his wife against the will of Troy . How then , my lord , could I be justly put to death . . . by you , with any show of right , seeing that he wedded me against my will , and those my other natural gifts have served a bitter slavery , instead of leading on to triumph ? If it is your will indeed

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: βούλῃ , τὸ χρῄζειν ἀμαθές ἐστί σου τόδε .
sections.translation: to master gods , that very wish displays your folly .

sections.label: Section 966
sections.original: βασίλειʼ , ἄμυνον σοῖς τέκνοισι καὶ πάτρᾳ
sections.translation: O my royal mistress , defend your children ’ s and your country ’ s cause , bringing to nothing her persuasive arguments , for she pleads well in spite of all her villainy ; this is monstrous !

sections.label: Section 969
sections.original: ταῖς θεαῖσι πρῶτα σύμμαχος γενήσομαι
sections.translation: First I will take up the cause of those goddesses ,

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: καὶ τήνδε δείξω μὴ λέγουσαν ἔνδικα .
sections.translation: and prove how she perverts the truth . For I can never believe that Hera or the maiden Pallas would have been guilty of such folly , the one to sell her Argos to barbarians , or that Pallas ever would make her Athens subject to the Phrygians ,

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: εἰ παιδιαῖσι καὶ χλιδῇ μορφῆς πέρι
sections.translation: coming as they did in mere wanton sport to Ida to contest the palm of beauty . For why should goddess Hera set her heart so much on such a prize ? Was it to win a nobler lord than Zeus ? or was Athena hunting down among the gods a husband ,

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ἣ παρθενείαν πατρὸς ἐξῃτήσατο ,
sections.translation: she who in her dislike of marriage won from her father the gift of remaining unwed ? Do not seek to impute folly to the goddesses , in the attempt to adorn your own sin ; never will you persuade the wise . Next you have said — what well may make men jeer — that Cypris came with my son to the house of Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν μένουσʼ ἂν ἥσυχός σʼ ἐν οὐρανῷ
sections.translation: Could she not have stayed quietly in heaven and brought you and Amyclae as well to Ilium ?

sections.label: Section 987
sections.original: ἦν οὑμὸς υἱὸς κάλλος ἐκπρεπέστατος ,
sections.translation: No ! my son was exceedingly handsome , and when you saw him your mind straight became your Aphrodite ; for every folly that men commit , they lay upon this goddess ,

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: καὶ τοὔνομʼ ὀρθῶς ἀφροσύνης ἄρχει θεᾶς .
sections.translation: and rightly does her name [*] begin the word for senselessness ; so when you caught sight of him in gorgeous foreign clothes , ablaze with gold , your senses utterly forsook you . Yes , for in Argos you had moved in simple state , but , once free of Sparta ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: χρυσῷ ῥέουσαν ἤλπισας κατακλύσειν
sections.translation: it was your hope to deluge by your lavish outlay Phrygia ’ s town , that flowed with gold ; nor was the palace of Menelaus rich enough for your luxury to riot in .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: εἶἑν · βίᾳ γὰρ παῖδα φῄς σʼ ἄγειν ἐμόν ·
sections.translation: Enough of this ! My son carried you off by force , so you say ; what Spartan saw this ? what cry for help

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἀνωλόλυξας — Κάστορος νεανίου
sections.translation: did you ever raise , though Castor was still alive , a vigorous youth , and his brother also , not yet among the stars ? Then when you had come to Troy , and the Argives were on your track , and the mortal combat had begun , whenever tidings came to you of

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: Μενέλαον ᾔνεις , παῖς ὅπως λυποῖτʼ ἐμὸς
sections.translation: Menelaus ’ prowess , you would praise him , to grieve my son , because he had so powerful a rival in his love ; but if the Trojans prospered , Menelaus was nothing to you . Your eye was fixed on Fortune , and by such practice you were careful to follow in her steps , careless of virtue ’ s cause .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: κἄπειτα πλεκταῖς σῶμα σὸν κλέπτειν λέγεις
sections.translation: And then you assert that you tried to let yourself down from the towers by stealth with twisted cords , as if unwilling to stay ? Where were you ever found fastening the noose about your neck , or whetting the knife , as a noble wife would have done in regret for her former husband ?

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: καίτοι σʼ ἐνουθέτουν γε πολλὰ πολλάκις ·
sections.translation: And yet often I advised you saying , Get away , daughter ; my sons will take other brides , and I will belp you to steal away , and convey you to the Achaean fleet ; oh , end the strife between us and Hellas ! But this was bitter to you .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ἐν τοῖς Ἀλεξάνδρου γὰρ ὕβριζες δόμοις
sections.translation: For you were wantoning in Alexander ’ s house , wishing to have obeisance done you by barbarians . Yes , it was a proud time for you ; and now after all this you have adorned yourself , and come forth and have dared to appear under the same sky as your husband , revolting wretch !

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἣν χρῆν ταπεινὴν ἐν πέπλων ἐρειπίοις ,
sections.translation: Better if you had come in tattered raiment , cowering humbly in terror , with hair cut short , and if your feeling for your past sins were one of shame rather than effrontery . Menelaus , hear the conclusion of my argument ;

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: στεφάνωσον Ἑλλάδʼ ἀξίως τήνδε κτανὼν
sections.translation: crown Hellas by slaying her as she deserves , and establish this law for all other women : death to every one who betrays her husband .

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: Μενέλαε , προγόνων τʼ ἀξίως δόμων τε σῶν
sections.translation: Avenge yourself , Menelaus , on your wife , as is worthy of your home and ancestors ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ψόγον τὸ θῆλύ τʼ , εὐγενὴς ἐχθροῖς φανείς .
sections.translation: clear yourself from the reproach of effeminacy at the lips of Hellas , and let your foes see your spirit .

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: ἐμοὶ σὺ συμπέπτωκας ἐς ταὐτὸν λόγου ,
sections.translation: Your thoughts coincide with mine , that she , without constraint , left my palace , and sought a stranger ’ s bed , and now Cypris is introduced for mere bluster . Away to those who shall stone you ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: πόνους τʼ Ἀχαιῶν ἀπόδος ἐν μικρῷ μακροὺς
sections.translation: and by your speedy death requite the weary toils of the Achaeans , so that you may learn not to bring shame on me !

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: μή , πρός σε γονάτων , τὴν νόσον τὴν τῶν θεῶν
sections.translation: Oh , by your knees , I implore you , do not impute that heaven-sent affliction to me , or slay me ; forgive me !

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: μηδʼ οὓς ἀπέκτεινʼ ἥδε συμμάχους προδῷς ·
sections.translation: Do not betray your allies , whose death this woman caused ;

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ἐγὼ πρὸ κείνων καὶ τέκνων σε λίσσομαι .
sections.translation: on their behalf , and for my children ’ s sake , I entreat you .

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: παῦσαι , γεραιά · τῆσδε δʼ οὐκ ἐφρόντισα .
sections.translation: Peace , revered lady ; to her I pay no heed . I bid my servants take her away , aboard the ship , in which she is to sail .

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: μή νυν νεὼς σοὶ ταὐτὸν ἐσβήτω σκάφος .
sections.translation: Oh never let her set foot within the same ship as you .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι ; μεῖζον βρῖθος ἢ πάροιθʼ ἔχει ;
sections.translation: Why is that ? is she heavier than before ?

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐραστὴς ὅστις οὐκ ἀεὶ φιλεῖ .
sections.translation: The one who loves once , must love always .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: ὅπως ἂν ἐκβῇ τῶν ἐρωμένων ὁ νοῦς .
sections.translation: Why , that depends how those we love are minded . But your wish shall be granted ; she shall not set foot upon the same ship with me ; for your advice is surely sound ;

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: ἐλθοῦσα δʼ Ἄργος ὥσπερ ἀξία κακῶς
sections.translation: and when she comes to Argos she shall die a shameful death as is her due , and impress the need of chastity on all women . No easy task ; yet shall her fate strike their foolish hearts with terror , even though they are more lost to shame than she . Exit Menelaus , dragging Helen with him .

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: οὕτω δὴ τὸν ἐν Ἰλίῳ
sections.translation: So then you have delivered into Achaea ’ s hand , O Zeus , your shrine in Ilium and your fragrant altar , the offerings of burnt sacrifice with smoke of myrrh to heaven uprising ,

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: πνὸν καὶ Πέργαμον ἱερὰν
sections.translation: and holy Pergamos , and glens of Ida tangled with the ivy ’ s growth , where rills of melting snow pour down their flood , a holy sun-lit land that bounds the world

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: τὰν καταλαμπομέναν ζαθέαν θεράπναν .
sections.translation: and takes the god ’ s first rays !
